headlineSummary: HN sentiment was positive across all three providers, with Anthropic drawing the strongest praise, OpenAI also viewed favorably, and Google Gemini showing limited evidence in a close tie with OpenAI [E1][E2][E5][E7].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini had a positive but lightly supported showing, with praise for strong intelligence and a helpful reverse-engineering workflow, and its result was in a close tie with OpenAI [E5][E9].
OpenAI was viewed positively overall, with comments praising ChatGPT/Codex for search, quality, and access, though one remark criticized recent tradeoffs [E2][E3][E4][E6].

Anthropic drew the warmest response, with Claude praised as a workhorse, a major productivity boost, and especially effective in debugging, reverse engineering, and code assistance [E1][E2][E4][E7][E8][E10].

evidence.excerpt: Claude is a space to think

annotations.rationale: The title presents Claude in affirming, promotional terms.

evidence.excerpt: ChatGPT is great for chatbots and the like, but, as was discussed in a prior thread, chatbots aren't the end-all-be-all of AI or LLMs. Claude Code is the workhorse for me and most folks I know for AI assisted development and business automation type tasks.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says ChatGPT is great for chatbots.
Calling Claude Code the workhorse for the commenter and peers is strong praise for usefulness and effectiveness.

evidence.excerpt: From my previous experiences, ChatGPT is just way better at deep searches through the internet than Claude.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says ChatGPT is way better for deep internet searches.
Claude is explicitly judged worse than ChatGPT for this use case.

evidence.excerpt: they massively controlled costs, but did so at the expense of intelligence, responsiveness, curiosity, skills, and all the things I've valued in O3. This was the point I dumped openai, and went with claude.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says OpenAI reduced valued qualities and explicitly says they dumped OpenAI.
The commenter says they switched to Claude after abandoning OpenAI, implying a favorable view of Claude.

evidence.excerpt: Even the best local models (ones you run on beefy 128GB+ RAM machines) get nowhere close to the sheer intelligence of Claude/Gemini/Codex.

annotations.rationale: Claude is explicitly praised as far more intelligent than the best local models.
Gemini is explicitly included among models with superior intelligence.
Codex is explicitly included among models with superior intelligence.

evidence.excerpt: If you have a ChatGPT Plus or Pro account, Codex is surprisingly just as good and comes with a much higher quota.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Codex is just as good and has a much higher quota, praising both quality and access.

evidence.excerpt: I do a lot of kubernetes ops with workloads I'm unfamiliar with (and not directly responsible for) and often give claude read access in order to help me debug things, including with things like a grafana skill in order to access the same monitoring tools humans have. It's saved me dozens of hours in the last months - and my job is significantly less frustrating now.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude saved dozens of hours and made their job significantly less frustrating.

evidence.excerpt: With Claude code and another Ghidra MCP I was able to reverse engineer a ransomware encryptor and decryptor (had both) to create a much more reliable version of the decryptor. Saved terabytes of data. Felt like a super power!

annotations.rationale: The commenter credits Claude Code with a major successful reverse-engineering task and describes the outcome in very enthusiastic terms.

evidence.excerpt: Passing a single function disassembly + Ghidra decompiler output + external symbol definitions RAG-style to an agent with a good system prompt does wonders even with inexpensive models such as Gemini 3 Flash.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Gemini 3 Flash does wonders in this reverse-engineering workflow.

evidence.excerpt: Claude Code helped decode and convert obsolete Dynamix/Torque3D file formats (improving existing Blender addons that were incomplete), got TorqueScript running in the browser, wrote shaders, and generally helped figure out what the original C++ code was doing.

annotations.rationale: The commenter lists multiple substantial ways Claude Code materially helped a preservation project.
